english: 1. lit. a bird's wing. — 2. a part of the roof or vertical projection of the same, a kind of façade, admitting of pictorial decoration Glr.
Sch., a winged diabolical creature, harpy.

(acc. to Liś. corrupted from व्यञ्जन) seasoning, condiment, sauce, in a legend; prob. also in a gen. sense: meat, food, byá-nai yo-byád (bya na'i yo byad) Lex., byá-na-ma (bya na ma), prob. id.; tsá-ba byá-na-ma jíg kyér-nas (tsha ba bya na ma zhig khyer nas) bringing some warm food Mil.
raven, or some similar bird S.g.; bya-nag-rdó-rje (bya nag rdo rje) Mil. id., because the raven is said to reach an age of a thousand years.

1. cock, the male of the domestic fowl, more definitely: byá(-po) mtsa-lu (bya po mtsha lu) B. and col.; byá-po dáṅ-po (bya po dang po), gnyis-pa (gnyis pa) etc., the first, the second cock-crow C. — 2. *byá-po skyá-po* (*bya po skya po*) W. sparrow. — 3. bya-po-tsi-tsí (bya po tsi tsi) Med., a medicinal plant, stopping the monthly courses; in Lh. the great balsamine, Impatiens Roylei.
down (feather), byai spu (bya'i spu) B. and col.
cock, the male of any bird.
1. a young bird. — 2. a young fowl, chicken.

1. inf. and part. fut. of byéd-pa (byed pa), q. v. — 2. sbst. deed, action, work, without any reference to time, jíg-rtén-gyi byá-ba ('jig rten gyi bya ba) and čós-kyi byá-ba (chos kyi bya ba) secular and religious works, frq.; mai byá-ba byéd-pa (ma'i bya ba byed pa) to act as a mother, to perform a mother's part Tar.; byá-ba zín-pa (bya ba zin pa) an action completely past Gram.; byá-ba maṅ yaṅ 'brás-bu čúṅ-ba (bya ba mang yang 'bras bu chung ba) much labour and little fruit, much work and little profit Tar.; der rgyál-po daṅ blón-po-rnams-kyi byá-ba byúṅ-ba yín (der rgyal po dang blon po rnams kyi bya ba byung ba yin) then the affairs of the kings and their officers, the concerns of the state and its functionaries, gained ground; also in an absolute sense byá-ba = secularity, worldliness, byá-ba btáṅ-ba jíg rgyán-du čé (bya ba btang ba zhig rgyan du che) a resigning of worldly things is fraught with great blessing Mil.; bya-byéd (bya byed) the doing, doings: bya-byéd nyúṅ-ba jíg rgyán-du čé (bya byed nyung ba zhig rgyan du che) the doing little brings great blessing, and so in a similar manner: byá-rgyu byéd-rgyu ma maṅ jíg (bya rgyu byed rgyu ma mang zhig) do not give way to a bustling disposition Mil., i.e. do not permit your contemplative state to be interrupted by a distracting activity of your mind; bstán-pa-la (bstan pa la) (or bstán-pai (bstan pa'i)) byá-ba byás-pai lo-rgyús (bya ba byas pa'i lo rgyus) an account of what has been done for the spread of the doctrine Tar.; byá-ba daṅ ˚brél-ba (bya ba dang 'brel ba) seems to be a grammatical term relating to the verb.
one free from business, one that has renounced all worldly employment, an ascetic, Ld.-Glr.
a tea-pot shaped vessel used in sacrificing.
1. the female of any kind of birds. — 2. hen, female fowl, also in conjunction with mtśá-lu (mtsha lu), cf. byá-po (bya po) (?).
Sch., bya-rmyéṅ (bya rmyeng) (another reading snyeṅ (snyeng)) byéd-pa (byed pa), to yawn Mil.
